meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: A Case Study for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how to use AI-driven debugging with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship projects faster and impress your clients today.
content.answer_block: To solve debugging bottlenecks, shift from manual tracing to AI-assisted analysis. By integrating Antigravity for architectural visualization and Claude Code for iterative repairs, you convert hours of frustration into minutes of precision, ensuring your client projects remain profitable, scalable, and error-free regardless of your current skill level.
content.intro:
You are staring at the monitor at 2:00 AM. The client needs the dashboard finished by morning, but a silent, elusive bug is crashing the authentication flow. You’ve refactored the same function four times, and the error logs are just cryptic noise. This is the 'freelancer's wall'—where progress dies and profitability evaporates.

But what if you stopped fighting the code and started orchestrating it? Modern AI agents like Claude Code and Antigravity don't just find syntax errors; they understand your architectural intent. By treating your codebase as a living entity rather than a wall of text, you can move from 'stuck' to 'shipped' in a fraction of the time.

steps.title: Map the Architectural Flow
steps.description: Before changing a single line, visualize your dependency graph to see where the logic breaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project directory and generate a dependency map highlighting the authentication flow. Identify potential circular dependencies.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scan the logs and pinpoint the exact source of the runtime crash.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Examine the latest stack trace in the error logs and cross-reference it with the auth service logic.

steps.title: Simulate and Verify
steps.description: Run a dry-run fix in a sandbox environment to ensure the patch doesn't create side effects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a fix for the identified auth bug that maintains compatibility with existing database schemas.

steps.title: Execute the Patch
steps.description: Apply the fix and automatically trigger a suite of regression tests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply the verified fix to auth.js and update the associated test files.

steps.title: Final Documentation
steps.description: Generate a clean commit message and update the README so the client understands the fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the bug, the root cause, and the applied fix in a professional format for the client.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit -m "Fix: Resolved auth race condition during session initialization"

pro_tips: Always run your AI commands in a dedicated git branch so you can diff the changes manually before merging.
Use Antigravity to generate 'God Mode' documentation for new clients to establish immediate authority.
When Claude suggests a fix, ask it to 'explain the trade-offs' to ensure you aren't sacrificing security for speed.
Pre-emptively use AI to write unit tests for high-risk modules before you even start coding.
If the AI gets stuck, ask it to 'act as a senior staff engineer with 20 years of experience' to force a more architectural perspective.
content.next_steps: Integrate these tools into your CI/CD pipeline to automate the code review process and reduce your manual debugging time by another 30%.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs hold your business hostage. Implement these AI workflows today to double your velocity and reclaim your evenings. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
